author: codex
scope: Codex-only relation endpoint-shape rule-or-data triage artifact; deterministic local inspection only; no provider-backed commands, post-ingest LLM harnesses, materialize/export-jsonl/full validate/DB publish, durable data edits, code edits, LOG edits, manifest edits, submissions edits, retired.json edits, or canonical/generated edits

recent_work_not_repeated: next20 validator patch for operates_on_corridor entity->corridor
relation046 data cleanup for has_treatment claim->treatment_component
relation048 data cleanup for operated_by entity->event
relation049 data cleanup for metric-context endpoint-shape rows
relation050 data cleanup for implemented_by entity->entity
current_checkpoint_context.inspection_method: Read-only SQLite queries against data/canonical.db using the current RELATION_ENDPOINT_SHAPES table in packages/pipeline/src/records/relations.ts, plus read-only rg/sed inspection of existing submission journals, source blocks, and prior review notes. No validate/materialize/export/publish/provider command was run.

remaining_bucket_counts.triage_note: All remaining exact endpoint-shape buckets are singletons. The selected operated_by entity->entity row is the clearest next deterministic data cleanup because prior cleanup lanes explicitly kept operated_by entity->entity out of the validator, and this particular row is supported only by a Paratransit Report agenda heading rather than direct operator evidence.

recommended_data_cleanup.why_data_cleanup: The current row uses operated_by with entity endpoints. Current operated_by is intentionally scoped to project|route|corridor -> entity, and prior relation cleanup notes rejected broad operated_by entity->entity admission because the bucket mixes service, agency, hierarchy, and weak document-context assertions. This singleton's accepted evidence is only the meeting agenda heading 'iii. Paratransit Report', so it is too thin to justify either a validator rule patch or a source-specific replacement assertion.
recommended_data_cleanup.why_not_validator_rule_patch: Adding entity to RELATION_ENDPOINT_SHAPES.operated_by.subject would clear this row but would also validate broad entity->entity operator assertions, including service/program, agency hierarchy, vendor/operator, and loose document-context rows. Previous rule triage for operated_by corridor->entity explicitly kept operated_by entity->entity invalid. The current corpus already has more precise Access-A-Ride/NYCT structure through part_of_agency and operates relations from stronger sources, so this row is not evidence of a rule gap.

candidate_submissions_to_retire.retire_reason: Malformed endpoint shape and insufficient direct evidence: the submitted relation says Access-A-Ride is operated by NYCT, but the accepted evidence is only the Paratransit Report agenda heading in an NYCT/Bus committee metrics report.

proposed_semantic_replacement_direction.reason: No accepted replacement entry is recommended from this submission alone. The cited evidence block supports only that the source contains a Paratransit Report section, not a new NYCT-operated-by-Access-A-Ride relation or hierarchy assertion.

if_a_future_worker_requires_preservation.caution: Use only if additional same-source evidence beyond p001_c0011 is added to the replacement. Do not encode the current submitted description as part_of_agency or operates without stronger source text.

recommended_data_cleanup.replacement_collision_check: Read-only relation lookup found existing Access-A-Ride/NYCT semantic coverage in stronger sources. Adding a new part_of_agency or operates replacement for meeting_doc_91586 would be redundant and under-evidenced.

payload_summary.description: Access-A-Ride paratransit service is operated as part of NYCT

representative_records.triage_read: The statement may be true in the broader corpus, but this accepted relation has only an agenda-heading evidence ref. The row should not become the reason to allow operated_by entity->entity globally, and a replacement is not needed because stronger same-concept relations already exist.

source_and_evidence_summary.submission_raw_text: Paratransit Report - included in NYCT and Bus Committee Meeting

evidence_blocks.summary: Agenda heading for a Paratransit Report section.

nearby_context_blocks_not_used_as_relati.summary: Defines Access-A-Ride trips/ridership and notes ridership is presented by provider type.
Lists Access-A-Ride provider categories, including primary providers operated by contractors, brokers, e-hails, street hails, and voucher/enhanced broker service.

payload_summary.description: Paratransit service managed by MTA NYCT Department of Buses.
New York City Transit Authority as reported in 2023 Prompt Payment Annual Report

why_safe: Retiring the single malformed submission clears one endpoint-shape issue without changing validator behavior or generated outputs in this prep lane.
The row's only accepted evidence block is an agenda heading, so retire-only avoids converting weak evidence into a stronger agency-role or hierarchy assertion.
The Access-A-Ride and NYCT endpoint records remain materialized and are not modified.
More precise Access-A-Ride/NYCT relations already exist from stronger sources, so retiring this under-evidenced row does not remove the corpus's only semantic coverage for the relationship.
The recommendation preserves prior validator boundaries that kept operated_by entity->entity invalid after the operated_by corridor->entity scoped rule patch.
The selected bucket does not overlap next20, relation046, relation048, relation049, relation050, or relation037-relation050 cleanup work.

non_goals: Do not edit packages/pipeline/src/records/relations.ts or tests for next24.
Do not edit data/submission-overrides/retired.json in this prep lane.
Do not append replacement submissions in this prep lane.
Do not edit data/canonical/*.jsonl, data/canonical.db, data/materialized/*, wiki/*, LOG.md, manifests, campaign files, or generated-output files.
Do not run canonicalize, campaign dedup, identity-review, write, ask, schema, ontology-normalize, any provider/profile-backed command, materialize, export-jsonl, full validate, or DB publish.
guards.follow_up_worker_scope: Retire sub_9e51ef484cc836e5 only; no replacement submission is recommended unless the worker finds stronger same-source evidence than the current p001_c0011 agenda heading.

commands_run.purpose: Confirmed the worktree is heavily dirty and must not be disturbed.

commands_run.command: rg/sed over data/review_notes, data/submissions, and raw/sources/meeting_doc_91586
commands_run.purpose: Checked prior cleanup decisions, located the accepted submission, and summarized evidence.
